data.title: Twilio in focus as Morgan Stanley upgrades on improving execution
data.description:
Twilio (NYSE:TWLO) was in the spotlight on Monday as Morgan Stanley upgraded the communications software maker, citing improving execution.

Shares rose 3.5% in premarket trading.

“We are increasingly confident in crediting TWLO's execution toward double-digit growth and operating margin outperformance on back of increased cross-sell, supported by our recent checks,” analyst Meta Marshall wrote in a note to clients. “We see recent sell-off as overdone, providing an attractive entry point, and we upgrade to [Overweight].” Marshall raised her rating on Twilio to Overweight from Equal-Weight and upped her price target to $160 from $144.

Delving a little deeper, Marshall said the selloff in the stock after it reported fourth-quarter results last week was “overdone” and created an “attractive buying opportunity.” Twilio shares now trade at roughly 19 times 2026 free cash flow estimates, well below the peer average of about 24.5 times estimates.

And with recent checks and the exit growth rate from the fourth-quarter showing improvement, Marshall said she has confidence in Twilio's ability to execute the plan it laid out at its analyst day.

“We are encouraged that management continues to run business around double-digit growth in terms of opex investments required, leaving potential for upside to margins if reacceleration we saw exiting 2024 is sustainable,” Marshal wrote.

“Our base case now contemplates achievement of management targets (21-22% operating margins by FY27) a year ahead, driven by revenue upside, Segment efficiencies, moves to lower cost geographies, increasing use of AI internally and product mix shift. On the latter, we continue to expect Segment to achieve operating break-even by 2Q25, and continued traction in sales motions and new features should continue to support profitability upside.”

data.title: FiscalNote divests additional non-core assets for $40M, shares jump
data.description:
Shares of FiscalNote Holdings (NYSE:NOTE) jumped 16% premarket on Monday after the company agreed to divest two portfolio companies within its Global Intelligence business, Oxford Analytica and Dragonfly, to Dow Jones, a division of News Corp (NASDAQ:NWSA), for total consideration of $40M.

The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2025, subject to receipt of antitrust clearance in Austria and other customary closing conditions. Dow Jones's parent company, News Corp (NASDAQ:NWSA), expects to receive a $4M tax benefit in connection with the transaction.

The resulting net proceeds provide FiscalNote a return of capital that helps reduce its senior term loan and strengthens its balance sheet while further simplifying the business as the company continues its ongoing strategic shift to focus on its core Policy platform.

“This divestiture enables us to pay down a significant portion of our senior term loan, resulting in a cumulative paydown of more than 60 percent in the past year. Beyond strengthening our financial position, this move will streamline operations, expand Adjusted EBITDA margins, and accelerate our path to positive free cash flow,” FiscalNote CEO Josh Resnik said.

Oxford Analytica and Dragonfly are each primarily based in the U.K. The businesses have offered their products independently of FiscalNote’s core products.

Oxford Analytica, acquired by FiscalNote in February 2021, produces geopolitical analysis and provides advisory services, while Dragonfly, acquired in January 2023, provides forward-looking global security intelligence and data.

Separately, FiscalNote has engaged John Lee, former head of contracts for Palantir’s U.S. Government Vertical, as a senior strategic advisor to support the implementation of its AI & tech solutions.

data.title: Strategy completes $2B offering of convertible senior notes
data.description:
• Bitcoin treasury company Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) has completed the $2B offering of its 0% convertible senior notes due March 1, 2030, to institutional buyers.
  • MSTR shares were trading +1.02% Monday pre-market at $302.75.
  • The company had announced the offering on February 20.
  • The noteholders will have the right to convert the notes only upon the occurrence of certain events before December 3, 2029.
  • Strategy said it will settle conversions by paying or delivering cash and shares of its class A common stock.
  • The initial conversion rate is 2.3072 shares of class A common stock per $1,000 principal amount of notes, representing a conversion price of ~$433.43 per share.
  • The notes can be redeemed for cash at the company's option at any time, and from time to time, on or after March 5, 2027, and on or before the 20th scheduled trading day immediately before the maturity date.
  • The redemption is subject to the criteria that the last reported sale price per share of the class A common stock exceeds 130% of the conversion price for a specified period of time and certain other conditions are satisfied.
  • The noteholders may require the company to repurchase the notes on March 1, 2028, at a cash repurchase price equal to the principal amount of the notes to be repurchased, plus accrued and unpaid special and additional interest, if any, to, but excluding, the repurchase date.

data.title: Apollo to acquire Bridge Investment Group for $1.5 billion
data.description:
• Apollo (NYSE:APO) and Bridge Investment Group (NYSE:BRDG) on Monday said they have signed a definitive agreement for Apollo to acquire Bridge in an all-stock transaction with an equity value of approximately $1.5 billion.
  • As per the terms of the transaction, Bridge stockholders and Bridge OpCo unit holders will receive 0.07081 shares of Apollo stock for each share of Bridge class A common stock and each Bridge OpCo class A common unit valued by the parties at $11.50 per each share, respectively.
  • As part of the deal, Bridge Executive Chairman Bob Morse will become an Apollo partner and head of its real-estate equity business.
  • Upon the closing of the transaction, Bridge will operate as a standalone platform within Apollo’s asset management business, retaining its existing brand, management team and dedicated capital formation team.
  • The company said Bridge Investment manages approximately $50 billion of high-quality AUM in real estate products targeting both institutional and wealth clients and is expected to be highly synergistic with Apollo’s existing real estate equity strategies and real estate credit platform.
  • Bridge will no longer be holding its fourth quarter and full-year 2024 earnings conference call and webcast scheduled for February 25, 2025, due to the pending transaction.
  • APO +0.29% premarket to $150.99.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Houston American Energy expands into renewable fuels with Abundia Global Impact deal
data.description:
Houston American Energy (NYSE:HUSA) +1.5% pre-market Monday after saying it agreed to acquire Abundia Global Impact Group, a company specializing in converting waste into high value fuels and chemicals, for an undisclosed sum.

Under the deal terms, Houston American (NYSE:HUSA) will acquire 100% of AGIG's issued and outstanding units from AGIG's members and will issue to AGIG's members a number of HUSA common shares equaling 94% of its aggregate issued and outstanding common stock at the time of the closing.

AGIG is preparing to build its first advanced plastic recycling facility in Cedar Port, Texas, which it said will become the hub for its five-year development plan in the U.S., designed to scale production capacity while maintaining capital discipline.

The two companies said they will continue to work towards a structured integration and execution plan, with additional updates expected in the coming months.

data.title: Airtel to offer Apple TV+, Apple Music to its Wi-Fi, postpaid customers
data.description:
• India's Bharti Airtel said it will offer Apple TV+ streaming services and Apple Music to some of its customers in India.
  • The firm said it has entered into a strategic partnership with Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), which allows all Home Wi-Fi customers on plans starting at INR 999 to get access to Apple TV+.
  • The company added postpaid customers on plans starting at INR 999 will get access to Apple TV+ and can avail six months of free Apple Music.

data.title: Juggernaut Exploration announces share consolidation
data.description:
• Juggernaut Exploration (TSXV:JUGR:CA) on Monday said that it will consolidate its common shares on a 10-old-for-1-new share basis.
  • The 143M shares currently issued and outstanding will be reduced to about 14.3M post-consolidation shares.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Connexa Sports Technologies skyrockets on content creation agreement with TikTok
data.description:
• Connexa Sports Technologies (NASDAQ:YYAI) on Monday said that its majority-owned subsidiary, Yuanyu Enterprise Management (YYEM), has signed an multi-channel network agency services agreement to create and sell content to TikTok as part of its exciting new vertical centered on social networking applications.
  • Under this agreement, YYEM will procure the production of content to be live-streamed or served as videos to TikTok’s multitude of users in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA), which will include engaging broadcasts across various categories, such as sports, gaming, and lifestyle topics, produced by popular Twitch hosts and other influencers within the network that YYEM is developing.
  • The company expects YYEM’s new vertical to also include live-streaming, voice chat rooms, gaming, and influencer-driven user-generated content (UGC). The fees generated by the arrangement with TikTok will depend on the rate of conversion by TikTok end-users, the company said.
  • YYAI +64.03% premarket to $1.05.
  • Source: Press Release
